palette: Slate grey, limestone cream, oxidized copper green, and deep navy.
lighting: Late afternoon Blue Hour transition; warm orange streetlights flickering against cool blue natural light.
instagram_prompts: Micro-perspective from a wrought iron balcony railing. Looking down at the Rue Réaumur in Paris. Cream-colored Haussmann buildings with slate grey roofs. A lone bistro table on the sidewalk below. Palette of limestone, slate, and navy. Warm streetlights starting to glow. High-detail textures of stone and iron. World is immense. FPP sensor capture.

First-person perspective (FPP) from inside a dimly lit room at Hotel National des Arts et Métiers. Looking through a tall window at the Parisian skyline at dusk. A small brass desk lamp is on, casting a warm glow on a rough limestone interior wall. Outside, the sky is a deep twilight navy. No person visible. Feeling of quiet, focused observation.

FPP macro shot of a worn stone gargoyle on the edge of a nearby building, seen from a high-altitude nanobot perspective. The stone is weathered, with traces of green oxidized copper nearby. In the background, the blurred lights of a Paris cafe terrace. Cool blue evening light with a single spark of warm orange from a street lamp. Impossibly large world.
